purpose: Freeze thesis-grade admissibility constraints before any rebuilt RQ3 small benchmark is accepted for paid API validation.
hard_blocks: Do not reuse rq3_small_v2.x as a formal benchmark without passing these rules.
Do not advance to mid or full while local probes still show large-scale shortcut solvability.
Do not use closed-set action descriptions as the only evidence that separates methods.
Do not accept a benchmark where RCA and E2E collapse because the prompt itself exposes the action taxonomy.

official_eval_prompt: Root-cause family and remediation must be elicited in open text first.
If closed-set labels are shown at all, they must be anonymized or introduced only after open-text reasoning is produced.
Action descriptions must not restate the same lexical anchors that already appear in the selected alert.

Hadoop: forcibly closed by the remote host

agent_vs_rag: Agent information must not be a strict subset of rag information for most cases.
Graph evidence must add a distinct signal not already inferable from the selected alert template alone.
reference_rules: Reference sets should vary across cases and noise levels when the underlying evidence changes.
References should not be near-duplicates of the evaluation alert.
At least one dataset should expose clear graph utility beyond retrieved lexical neighbors.

family_design: Every family must have at least two plausible actions or sub-decisions in the curated small.
No family should be represented only by one canonical lexical trigger.

dataset_priorities.HDFS: Bias toward weaker pipeline or transfer-stage alerts instead of direct serving and allocation anchors.
dataset_priorities.OpenStack: Re-expand beyond image-chain vs instance-state only; include host compatibility or control-service cases if they can be made support-complete without direct label leakage.
dataset_priorities.Hadoop: Prefer longer contexts and weaker selected alerts; if the current source pool cannot support this, reset the pool instead of patching prompts.

interpretation: If open alert-only probing is already high, the benchmark is still too easy.
If closed-set vanilla remains near perfect at high noise, the prompt contract is still over-helpful.
If agent, rag, and vanilla remain tied locally, there is still no defensible reason to spend paid API budget.
